nameMeanings.name: Squire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name, occupational name, or a nickname from Middle English squyer, squer, skier, sker, northern Middle English swire (Old French escuyer, escuier) ‘shield bearer, esquire, personal attendant to a knight; landowner below the rank of knight; young man of gentle birth; household attendant, retainer, page, messenger’. Many early bearers of this name were of modest means and cannot have been members of the gentry; in such cases the name may have been used hyperbolically for a low-ranking servant or as a nickname. By the 17th century, the term denoted any member of the landed gentry, but this is unlikely to have influenced the development of the surname.

variants.name: Swire
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Squire  , from a northern form of the word.

variants.name: Quire
meanings: Scottish or Irish: shortened form of   McQuire   (see   McGuire  ).

variants.name: Squires
meanings: English: variant of Squire , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Scuderi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmelo, Salvatore, Angelo, Carlo, Gaetano, Vito, Antonio, Dante, Mario, Rocco, Rosario, Antonino, Biaggio, Eduardo, Ermelinda, Margarita.

Italian (Sicily): status name equivalent to Squire , from scudiero, scutiero, dialect scuteri ‘shieldbearer’ (from medieval Latin scutarius, an agent derivative of scutum ‘shield’). In Byzantium, the Greek word skoutarios denoted a member of the imperial guard.

variants.name: Square
meanings: English (Devon) and Scottish (Lanarkshire): variant of   Squire  .

nameMeanings.name: Charlotte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine diminutive of   Charles  , used in England since the 17th century. It was particulary popular in the 18th and 19th centuries, in part due to the influence of firstly  Queen Charlotte  (  1744–1818 ), wife of  George III , and secondly the novelist  Charlotte Brontë  (  1816–55 ); it has again come to prominence since the 1980s, especially in England and Australia.

variants.name: Charlotta
meanings: feminine diminutive of   Charles  , used in England since the 17th century. It was particulary popular in the 18th and 19th centuries, in part due to the influence of firstly  Queen Charlotte  (  1744–1818 ), wife of  George III , and secondly the novelist  Charlotte Brontë  (  1816–55 ); it has again come to prominence since the 1980s, especially in England and Australia.

variants.name: Charlette
meanings: feminine diminutive of   Charles  , used in England since the 17th century. It was particulary popular in the 18th and 19th centuries, in part due to the influence of firstly  Queen Charlotte  (  1744–1818 ), wife of  George III , and secondly the novelist  Charlotte Brontë  (  1816–55 ); it has again come to prominence since the 1980s, especially in England and Australia.

nameMeanings.name: Mackrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Old French makerel, maquerel ‘mackerel’ (the sea fish).
English: perhaps a nickname from Old French maquerel ‘brothel-keeper, broker, pimp’ but this is less likely than 1.
Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic MacFhearghail (compare Farrell ). The name may have been confused with Norman use of the name in 1 above.

variants.name: McRill
meanings: Probably a Gaelicized spelling of English Mackrill, avariant of Mackrell .

variants.name: Farrell
meanings: Mainly North American: transferred use of the surname, originally a Norman baronial name (d'Airelle) borne by a family who came from Airelle in Calvados. It was first used as a given name towards the end of the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: Charlotte Mackrell Squire was born in 1565, in Charlton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. She married Henry Squire about 1586. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. She died in 1660, at the age of 95.

fr.lifeSummary: Charlotte Mackrell Squire est née en 1565, à Charlton, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle a épousé Henry Squire environ 1586. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1660, à l’âge de 95 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Charlotte Mackrell Squire wurde 1565 in Charlton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hat um 1586 Henry Squire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1660 im Alter von 95 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Charlotte Mackrell Squire è nata il nel 1565 nel Charlton, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Lei sposato Henry Squire circa 1586. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1660, all'età di 95 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Charlotte Mackrell Squire родилась в 1565 г. в местности чарлтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Squire около 1586 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1660 г. в возрасте 95 лет.
